title: Komm Susser Jude
wikitext:
[]: Komm Susser Jude             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Mar 06, 2011 4:47 pm Apols if this has been done before, but i just found this that has been uploaded a few days ago http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l_Ociwi-1cc

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Mar 06, 2011 10:01 pm Relevant

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Mar 06, 2011 10:46 pm They're similar in structure, but no more so than other musical works would be similar to each other. EDIT: I wouldn't be surprised if this song was used as temp score for the scene, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Sun Mar 06, 2011 11:13 pm They are intensely similar. Komm Suesser Tod is essentially Hey Jude with a smattering of A Whiter Shade of Pale, stirred together and baked at a hundred and fifty degrees until the lyrics are nice and suicidal.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Mar 06, 2011 11:52 pm Damn, I was hoping for a mash-up remix putting the two songs together.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 07, 2011 1:07 am esselfortium wrote: Damn, I was hoping for a mash-up remix putting the two songs together.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Mar 07, 2011 7:54 am Yeah sorry its not a an acutal mash-up but it demonstrates not only how the 2 songs are similar but also how Hey Jude fits nicely with the images in places - particularly as it soars off into the 'na na na na' section as Maya has her moment with Ritsuko. Ill try and find an instrumental of KST and an acapella of Hey Jude and ill see what i can do on the mash-up side of things.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Mar 07, 2011 9:23 am ^ Yeah, the two songs really don't show their similarities until the very end. Most of the stuff beforehand was just similar tempo, instruments, and orchestration, which the same can be said for a lot of the similarities of modern music. ("Hey, that electronic beat sounds like every other electronic beat out there...")

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Mar 07, 2011 3:24 pm You know, if someone does some fine tuning on the editing, I bet that someone will win an AMV contest lock, stock, and barrel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 07, 2011 10:25 pm Okay, so I wasted a little time and used my meager music-editing skills to splice the intros. Proof of concept, if nothing else. http://evageeks.org/images/reichu/komm_susser_jude_intro.mp3 I'm not sure how the rest of the song would be approached. A duet, maybe? And here's a super-shitty mash-up of the outros. (Complete with a distracting editing fuckup around 0:28 that I don't feel like dealing with.) I tried to match the tempos up best I could, but beyond that my skills are pretty much nonexistent. http://evageeks.org/images/reichu/komm_susser_jude_outro.mp3

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Mar 07, 2011 10:26 pm Don't tease us, Reichi-kun! That was awesome!! Do the whole song!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Mar 08, 2011 7:28 am Those strings on KST outro go REALLY well

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 08, 2011 7:51 am Jayfive wrote: Those strings on KST outro go REALLY well

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Mar 08, 2011 8:52 am 'remember to let her under your skin...' Brrr... One acapella of Hey Jude + KST + half an hour tickering on FL Studio = http://soundcloud.com/jayfive/komm-susser-jude-part-1 Theres maybe one beat slightly out but thats Ringo's drumming for you. Its worth noting that I had to make the minimum of pitch adjustments to make the 2 track's tempo match. Ill work on the rest in the coming days

[]:             Quote Post by Gydafud » Sun May 08, 2011 8:24 am Sorry for the bump, but Komm Susser Tod always seemed to remind me of the Crowded House song "Don't dream its over" Mainly this section. Edit: I guess linking to a specific time doesn't work. 1:55 in.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun May 08, 2011 9:23 am If you read the thread title in german, it translates perfectly into: "Come Sweet Jew"

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun May 08, 2011 9:45 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: If you read the thread title in german, it translates perfectly into: "Come Sweet Jew"

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sun May 08, 2011 10:51 am Reichu wrote: Okay, so I wasted a little time and used my meager music-editing skills to splice the intros. Proof of concept, if nothing else. http://evageeks.org/images/reichu/komm_susser_jude_intro.mp3 I'm not sure how the rest of the song would be approached. A duet, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Jun 04, 2011 10:22 pm http://tubedubber.com/#bCLoNOYcVQU:eDdI7GhZSQA:90:100:0:0:true Not the best, but the similarities are quite noticeable.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sat Oct 15, 2011 8:53 pm Reichu wrote: Okay, so I wasted a little time and used my meager music-editing skills to splice the intros. Proof of concept, if nothing else. http://evageeks.org/images/reichu/komm_susser_jude_intro.mp3 I'm not sure how the rest of the song would be approached. A duet, maybe?
